title: Egyptian Arab Land Bank (Egypt) Profile & Reviews
description: Explore a detailed profile and institutional data for Egyptian Arab Land Bank in Egypt. Share your experience or access institutional-grade data for professional insights.

faq.question: What is Egyptian Arab Land Bank?
faq.answer: Egyptian Arab Land Bank is a prominent Egyptian financial institution with a rich history dating back to the late 19th century. Originally established to support Egypt's agricultural sector, the bank has evolved into a comprehensive commercial bank offering a wide range of financial services. The bank maintains operations in Egypt, Jordan, and Palestine, serving as a specialized institution with focus on real estate investments and trade finance. With over 140 years of history, Egyptian Arab Land Bank is one of the oldest banking institutions in the Arab World. The bank has total assets exceeding 14 billion Egyptian pounds (approximately $2.6 billion) and operates 43 branches across its three markets. It is headquartered in Giza, Egypt.

faq.question: When was Egyptian Arab Land Bank founded?
faq.answer: The institution's origins trace back to February 15, 1880, when the Egyptian Land Bank was established to assist farmers and landowners in Egypt with financial support to boost agricultural productivity. This makes it one of the oldest financial institutions in the region. The bank underwent significant transformation in March 1946 when the Arab League, during its third session, issued a resolution to establish the Arab Land Bank through an Egyptian Royal Decree. It was registered as the Arab Land Company, an Egyptian shareholding company with headquarters in Cairo, on January 9, 1947. The company was initially created to support Palestinian agriculture and help Palestinians maintain land ownership. However, the 1948 situation in Palestine prevented the bank from continuing operations there, leading to re-registration in Jordan in 1951. In 1999, the Egyptian Cabinet issued a resolution changing the name to "Egyptian Arab Land Bank" to reflect the institution's vision and expanded role in supporting and developing Arab economies.

faq.question: Where is Egyptian Arab Land Bank headquartered?
faq.answer: Egyptian Arab Land Bank is headquartered in Giza, Egypt. The bank's main offices coordinate operations across its three-country network while maintaining its historical connection to Egypt's financial sector. From this headquarters, the bank manages its specialized operations in real estate investments and trade finance throughout Egypt, as well as its commercial banking activities in Jordan and Palestine.

faq.question: Where does Egyptian Arab Land Bank operate?
faq.answer: Egyptian Arab Land Bank operates across three countries in the Middle East: Egypt, Jordan, and Palestine. The bank maintains a total of 43 branches distributed throughout these markets. In Egypt, the bank operates multiple branches supporting its specialization in real estate investments and trade finance. In Jordan, the bank has 14 branches and functions as a commercial bank. In Palestine, the bank operates 7 branches plus an additional office in Jerusalem, providing comprehensive commercial banking services. The bank also maintains a network of correspondent banking relationships around the world to facilitate international transactions and services for its clients across the Arab region.

faq.question: What services does Egyptian Arab Land Bank offer?
faq.answer: Egyptian Arab Land Bank provides different service portfolios depending on the country of operation. In Egypt, the bank specializes in real estate investment services and trade finance, offering real estate loans, investment funds, advisory services, and various investment products. The bank's agricultural heritage continues through loans and financial services for farmers and agricultural landowners to boost productivity and income. Real estate services include financing for property purchases, investment funds, and development project financing. In Jordan and Palestine, the bank operates as a comprehensive commercial bank providing full retail, corporate, and investment banking services. These include savings accounts, current accounts, personal and business loans, credit cards, deposit products, certificates, and online banking platforms. Additional services across all markets include investment products, insurance services, trade finance solutions, cash management, and specialized lending programs. The bank also provides financial education and technical assistance to help clients make informed investment decisions.

faq.question: What makes Egyptian Arab Land Bank unique?
faq.answer: Egyptian Arab Land Bank's remarkable 144-year history distinguishes it as one of the oldest banking institutions in the Arab World. The bank's founding mission to support Arab communities—particularly in agriculture and land ownership—has shaped its identity and strategic focus throughout its evolution. The bank's expansion to Palestine in the 1940s demonstrated its commitment to serving Arab populations during critical historical periods. Although forced to relocate operations to Jordan in 1951, the bank maintained its mission of supporting Palestinian and Jordanian agriculture and economic development. Egyptian Arab Land Bank's dual operating model is unique: functioning as a specialized real estate and trade finance bank in Egypt while operating as a full-service commercial bank in Jordan and Palestine. This allows the institution to leverage its expertise in different markets while maintaining cohesive regional presence. The bank's 1999 name change to "Egyptian Arab Land Bank" formalized its pan-Arab identity and commitment to supporting economic development across Arab economies, not just within Egypt.

faq.question: What type of financial institution is Egyptian Arab Land Bank?
faq.answer: Egyptian Arab Land Bank operates under different classifications depending on the country. In Egypt, it is classified as a specialized commercial bank focusing on real estate investments and trade finance, regulated by the Central Bank of Egypt. In Jordan and Palestine, the bank functions as a comprehensive commercial bank providing full retail, commercial, and investment banking services to both individual and corporate clients. This dual structure allows the institution to serve different market needs while maintaining operational expertise in specialized and general banking. The bank is registered as a joint-stock company and operates under the regulatory frameworks of the Central Bank of Egypt for its Egyptian operations, with corresponding oversight from Jordanian and Palestinian banking authorities in those respective markets.
